task.title: Preserve multiline formatting for closures with statement bodies
task.summary: Closure compaction is flattening real statement blocks into one-liners.
task.problem: The formatter currently rejoins closure heads, bodies, and closing braces without checking whether the closure body is a single short expression or a multi-statement block. This breaks readability and interacts badly with nested if/else logic and let bindings inside closures.

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: Closures with multiple statements remain multiline.
Simple closures like each {|line| $line | str trim } may still compact when they fit.
catch, each, reduce, and similar closures with let or if statements inside them keep a structured multiline form.
